heroText: Ouagadougou or Wagadugu is the capital and largest city of Burkina Faso, and the administrative, communications, cultural and economic centre of the nation.  It has a population of 2,415,266 in 2019.

highlights.name: Ouagadougou
highlights.text: Ouagadougou or Wagadugu is the capital and largest city of Burkina Faso, and the administrative, communications, cultural and economic centre of the nation.

history.text: 28 people were killed, and at least 56 wounded; after a government counterattack, a total of 176 hostages were released the morning after the initial attack.
Foundation and regional importance Ouagadougou was founded possibly as early as 1050 by the Soninke Wangara diaspora from the Ghana Empire, also known as Wagadu.

history.label: 14th century
history.text: The Mossi people, moving north in the 14th century, conquered Wagadugu around the same time they raided Walata, contributing to the decline of the Mali Empire.

history.label: 15th century
history.text: The eponymous Wagadugu Kingdom was founded in the 15th century, which became the main center of the Mossi States around 1495.

history.label: 17th century
history.text: The 10th Moro Naba, Nyadfo, was the first Moro-Naba to live at Ouagadougou, in the middle of the 17th century.
On 5 September 1896 French forces entered Ouagadougou and burned the city to the ground.
In 1919 the colonial administration made Ouagadougou the capital of the Upper Volta territory, extensively rebuilding the town.

In 1954 the railroad line from Ivory Coast reached the city, spurring massive population growth. Independence On 15 January 2016, gunmen armed with heavy weapons attacked central Ouagadougou at the Cappuccino restaurant and the.
The jihadist insurgency continued with major attacks in 2017 and 2018.

history.label: Civic role
history.text: According to legend, the city was taken by Oubri, a grandson of Ouedraogo.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: It became the permanent capital under the 21st Moro Naba, Zombre, a century later.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: The Moro-Naba Ceremony is still performed every Friday by the Moro-Naba and his court.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: The 24th Moro Naba, Doulougou, built the first mosque in Ouagadougou early in the nineteenth century.
